sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see! LEFT moved the top two shapes left by 3 columns:
- Left L-shape: was at cols 18-26, now at cols 15-23 (moved left 3)
- Right top L-shape: was at cols 36-44, now at cols 39-47 (moved RIGHT 3? Wait, that's wrong...)
Wait, let me re-check. The left shape boundary was [[15, 18], [15, 26], ...] and now is [[15, 15], [15, 23], ...]. So it moved left by 3.
The right top shape boundary was [[15, 36], [15, 44], ...] and now is [[15, 39], [15, 47], ...]. So it moved RIGHT by 3.
So LEFT moves the left shape left and the right shape right - they move AWAY from each other horizontally! Similarly, UP moves both up and DOWN moves both down.
The yellow L-shape at bottom right stayed at cols 51-59, unchanged.
Let me now try to understand the goal. The shapes can move independently in each panel. Maybe the goal is to position them to match the yellow L-shape pattern, or to overlap in some way.
Actually wait - let me reconsider. The left panel has the black L with white dots. The right panel has two L-shapes: dark gray (top) and yellow (bottom). The yellow one doesn't move. Maybe the goal is to move the dark gray L to overlap/match the yellow L?
Let me try RIGHT to bring the shapes back and also understand the movement better.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# LEFT moves left-shape left, right-shape right (away from center)
# Let me try RIGHT to bring them back together
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
